summary: April 4, 2016, was marked by two major events that captured global attention. The Panama Papers leak, unveiled by the ICIJ and *Süddeutsche Zeitung*, exposed the use of offshore tax havens by numerous high-profile individuals, leading to widespread outrage and calls for reform. Meanwhile, in the world of sports, the Villanova Wildcats won the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship in a thrilling game against the North Carolina Tar Heels, highlighted by a last-second three-pointer. These stories underscored significant developments in both international finance and American sports history.

stories.headline: Panama Papers Leak Exposes Global Tax Evasion
stories.summary: The Panama Papers, a massive leak of 11.5 million documents from Mossack Fonseca, revealed how politicians, business leaders, and celebrities used offshore tax havens to hide wealth and evade taxes. The leak, published by the ICIJ and *Süddeutsche Zeitung*, led to global outrage, investigations, and policy changes. The documents detailed over 214,000 offshore companies, implicating numerous high-profile individuals.

stories.headline: Villanova Wins NCAA Title with Buzzer-Beater
stories.summary: On April 4, 2016, the Villanova Wildcats won the NCAA Men's Basketball Championship by defeating the North Carolina Tar Heels 77–74. The game ended with a dramatic buzzer-beating three-pointer by Kris Jenkins, securing Villanova's first national title since 1985. This thrilling finish is celebrated as one of the most memorable moments in college basketball history.
